title: Free vs. Paid Apps
drDigitalIntro:
Free and paid apps make money differently.
• Free apps may show ads or offer in-app purchases.
• Paid apps cost money upfront but usually have no ads.
• Compare both options before you decide — sometimes paying a few dollars saves you from constant interruptions.

playgroundTask.goal: You compared a free app with ads to a paid app without ads

steps.say: Click on the Games category to browse games.
Click on Bubble Pop — the free game. Look for the 'Ads' and 'In-app purchases' tags.
Notice the orange tags: Contains ads and In-app purchases. Go back to compare with a paid option.
Now click on Zen Garden — the paid game with no ads.
Notice the green 'No ads' tag and the $4.99 price. Install whichever you prefer — there's no wrong answer!

drDigitalSuccess: You compared a free app with ads to a paid app without ads. Both are valid choices — now you know what to look for!
drDigitalHint: Open each app listing to read the tags. 'Contains ads' means you'll see advertisements. 'No ads' means a clean experience.
